report_name: 'SleeperGem' Supply Chain Attack Hits RubyGems via Dormant Accounts

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
attack_patterns.description: Attackers published malicious versions of legitimate RubyGems packages by adding a malicious dependency, `git_credential_manager`.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: Attackers hijacked long-dormant maintainer accounts (e.g., 'LR-DEV', 'pinkroom') on RubyGems to publish malicious package versions.

attack_patterns.name: System Checks
attack_patterns.description: The malicious code included checks to determine if it was running in a CI/CD or automated build environment, aborting execution to evade automated security scanning and specifically target developer machines.

attack_patterns.name: Systemd Service
attack_patterns.description: The second-stage backdoor payload established persistence on developer machines by creating malicious systemd services.

attack_patterns.name: Cron
attack_patterns.description: The second-stage backdoor payload established persistence on developer machines by creating malicious cron jobs.

attack_patterns.name: Setuid and Setgid
attack_patterns.description: The backdoor attempted to escalate privileges to root by installing a setuid root shell at /usr/local/sbin/ping6.

malware.name: git_credential_manager
malware.description: A malicious Ruby gem (versions 2.8.0-2.8.3) impersonating Microsoft's Git Credential Manager. It acted as a first-stage dropper to deliver a persistent backdoor and was added as a dependency to other compromised gems.

malware.name: SleeperGem Backdoor
malware.description: A persistent, multi-stage backdoor installed by the git_credential_manager dropper. It establishes persistence via systemd and cron, and attempts to escalate privileges by creating a setuid root shell.

tools.name: Microsoft Git Credential Manager
tools.description: A legitimate Microsoft tool that was impersonated by the malicious `git_credential_manager` Ruby gem as part of a dependency confusion attack.

tools.name: Dendreo
tools.description: A legitimate Ruby gem that was compromised in versions 1.1.3 and 1.1.4 to include the malicious `git_credential_manager` gem as a dependency, thereby distributing the malware.

tools.name: fastlane-plugin-run_tests_firebase_testlab
tools.description: A popular, legitimate Ruby gem that was compromised in version 0.3.2 to include the malicious `git_credential_manager` gem as a dependency, significantly amplifying the attack's reach to over 574,000 downloads.

campaigns.name: SleeperGem
campaigns.description: A sophisticated software supply chain attack targeting the RubyGems ecosystem by hijacking dormant maintainer accounts to publish malicious versions of legitimate packages and distribute a persistent backdoor onto developer workstations.

identities.name: RubyGems
identities.description: The targeted software package registry for the Ruby programming language, which was compromised through the takeover of dormant maintainer accounts.

identities.name: Developers
identities.description: The intended victims of the SleeperGem attack, whose workstations were targeted for backdoor installation, credential theft, and potential lateral movement.

identities.name: LR-DEV
identities.description: A dormant RubyGems maintainer account that was hijacked by attackers to publish malicious packages as part of the SleeperGem campaign.

identities.name: pinkroom
identities.description: A dormant RubyGems maintainer account that was hijacked by attackers to publish malicious packages as part of the SleeperGem campaign.

infrastructure.name: SleeperGem Forgejo C2 Host
infrastructure.description: An attacker-controlled Forgejo (Git service) host used by early versions of the git_credential_manager dropper to fetch a second-stage payload.

indicators.name: SleeperGem Malicious RubyGem Installation
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: SleeperGem Malicious RubyGem Installation
id: f9a8b7c6-d5e4-4f3a-2b1c-0a9b8c7d6e5f
status: experimental
description: Detects the installation of the malicious 'git_credential_manager' Ruby gem, a component of the SleeperGem supply chain attack.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/sleepergem-uses-three-malicious.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-20
tags:
  - attack.t1195.001
  - attack.initial_access
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '/gem'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'install'
      - 'git_credential_manager'
  condition: selection

indicators.name: SleeperGem Setuid Root Shell Creation
indicators.pattern:
title: SleeperGem Setuid Root Shell Creation
id: e8d7c6b5-a4f3-4e2d-1c0b-9a8b7c6d5e4f
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of chmod to set the SUID bit on /usr/local/sbin/ping6, a technique used by the SleeperGem backdoor for privilege escalation.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/sleepergem-uses-three-malicious.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-20
tags:
  - attack.t1548.001
  - attack.privilege_escalation
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '/chmod'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'u+s'
      - '/usr/local/sbin/ping6'
  condition: selection

indicators.name: SleeperGem Systemd User Service Persistence
indicators.pattern:
title: SleeperGem Systemd User Service Persistence
id: d7c6b5a4-93e2-4d1c-0b9a-8b7c6d5e4f3a
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of a new service file in a user's systemd directory, a persistence technique used by the SleeperGem backdoor.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/sleepergem-uses-three-malicious.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-20
tags:
  - attack.t1543.002
  - attack.persistence
level: medium
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|contains: '/.config/systemd/user/'
    TargetFilename|endswith: '.service'
  condition: selection

indicators.name: SleeperGem Cron Job Persistence
indicators.pattern:
title: SleeperGem Cron Job Persistence
id: c6b5a493-82d1-4c0b-9a8b-7c6d5e4f3a2b
status: experimental
description: Detects suspicious modification of user crontab files, a persistence technique used by the SleeperGem backdoor.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/sleepergem-uses-three-malicious.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-20
tags:
  - attack.t1053.003
  - attack.persistence
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '/crontab'
  filter_legit:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - ' -l'
      - ' -r'
  condition: selection and not filter_legit

relationships.target_name: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
